title: Haplopteris anguste-elongata
intro: Haplopteris anguste-elongata, also known as the Chinese rudder fern or narrow elongated Haplopteris, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Adiantaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Haplopteris anguste-elongata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Haplopteris anguste-elongata is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Haplopteris anguste-elongata is a species of fern in the family Haplopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Haplopteris, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Haplopteris anguste-elongata is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Haplopteridales. Within the class Polypodiopsida, it is further divided into the subclass Polypodiidae. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
